folio.label: 115r
folio.l: Ne coude of it , the beaute wel deuyse
Ne Priapus , mygút nougút suffise
Thougú he be god , of gardeynes for to telle
The beaute of the gardeyn , and the welle
That stod vnder a laurer , alwey grene
Ful ofte tyme he , Pluto and his Quene
Proserpina , and al hire feyree
Disporten he~ , and maken melodee
A boute that welle , and daunsyngö as men told
¢ This noble knygút , Ianuarie the old
Swicú deynte hatú , in it to walke and pley
That he wol suffre , no wygút bere the key
But he hiö self , for of the smal wyket
He bar alwey , of siluere , a cleket
Witú whiche , whan that hi~ liste , he it vnshette
And whan he wold pay , his wyues dette
In somer sesou~ , thider wold he go
And May his wyf , and no wygút but they two
And thynges , whicú , that were nat don a bedde
He in the gardeyn , parfourmed he~ , and spedde
And in this wyse , ful many a mery day
Lyuetú this Ianuarie , and this fressú May
¢ But worldly ioye , may nougút alwey endure
To Ianuarie , ne to no creature
O sodeyne hap~ , o thow fortune vnstable
Lyke to the Skorpiou~ , so disceyuable
That flaterest witú thyn hede , whan æè wylt stynge
Thy tail is detú , thurgú thyn envenemynge
O brotel ioye , o swete venym queynte
O monstre , that so sotelly , canst peynte
Thy ©iftes , vnder hewe of stedfastnes
That thow disceyuest , bothe more and lesse
Why hast æè Ianuarie , thus disceyued
That haddest hi~ , for thy ful frend resceyued
And now æè hast byreft hi~ , bothe hise eyen
For sorwe of whicú , he desiretú to deyen
Allas this noble Ianuarie , the free
A mydde hise lust , and his prosperitee
Is waxen blynd , and that al sodeynly
He wepetú , he weyletú also , pitously
And there witú al , the fire of Ielousie
Leste that his wyf , shulde falle in som folie
So brent his hert , that he wolde fayn
That som man , bothe hire and hi~ had slayn
For neither after his detú , ne in his lyf
Ne wolde he , æat she were , no loue ne wyf
